freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe ticketing, refund, fee, and capacity values that can change at fork time without changing seat-before-payment ordering, ticket issuance, fraud fail-open, QR validation, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: How long a seat hold remains valid before payment completion.

boundedRange.rationale: Short holds reduce abandoned inventory while a minimum window keeps checkout usable.

keys.semantic: Refund request window before an event starts.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants can tighten or relax refund policy without changing refund audit persistence.

keys.semantic: Platform service-fee rate applied to ticket purchases.

boundedRange.rationale: Fee changes are tenant policy, while negative fees and extreme rates remain outside the safe adaptation surface.

keys.semantic: Payment processing fee rate used for checkout totals.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants can reflect processor contracts without changing payment state transitions.

keys.semantic: Whether event capacity changes require approval before taking effect.

boundedRange.rationale: The policy can differ by tenant; the adjustment record and capacity event remain mandatory.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-09 uses checkout, ticket list, check-in kiosk, refund queue, and QR confirmation views. Tenants may tune labels and policy values but must not remove payment, ticket, refund, or check-in state visibility.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Anonymous checkout, tenant-user ticket access, event-organiser check-in, and platform-admin refund operations are fixed for certification. Tenant adaptation may alter copy and policy, not role isolation.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant event-ticket policies that can be changed while fixed payment, ticketing, waitlist, and refund invariants remain intact.

edgeCases.name: Short seat hold for high-demand events
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can shorten the reservation hold during high-demand sales without allowing payment before the seat hold.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T99 reads the tenant-scoped TTL and stores the seat hold before payment processing is requested.

edgeCases.name: Generous refund window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can allow refunds farther from event start while preserving refund record persistence and compliance escalation.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T104 reads the refund window and stores refund records before emitting refund events.

edgeCases.name: Processor-specific fee policy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can tune fee rates to reflect its processor contract without changing ticket issuance or payment success criteria.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T112 is inline-pure and returns a fee breakdown without side effects.

edgeCases.name: Capacity approval policy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can require or skip approval for capacity changes while every adjustment remains stored and auditable.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T116 reads the policy, stores the capacity-adjustment record, and emits capacity.adjusted.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: T99 stores the seat hold before emitting payment.process.requested.
T99 treats full capacity as WAITLISTED rather than a thrown business exception.
T100 stores payment records before payment.processed is emitted.
T102 stores tickets before ticket.issued and ticket.purchase.completed are emitted.
T102 keeps the backward cross-wave to FLOW-04 RSVP handling guarded as not a plan exemplar.
T103 validates tickets server-side before storing a ticket-validation record.
T104 stores refund records before refund.initiated is emitted.
T105 uses retry and exhausted escalation paths without throwing for business outcomes.
T107 treats PENDING as a valid private booking state.
T108 group booking remains all-or-nothing inside a transaction.
T112 remains inline-pure with no database writes or queue events.
T113 fraud service outage fails open and emits a fraud check failed audit event.
T118 analytics failures do not block the purchase path.
All FLOW-09 handlers remain FLOW_SCOPED and return DataProcessResult.

tenant-a-fast-release-event-ticketing.description: Tenant A shortens abandoned-checkout holds and keeps a moderate refund window for paid networking events.
tenant-a-fast-release-event-ticketing.impact: Seats release faster when checkout is abandoned, refunds remain available until 48 hours before event start, and payment/ticket ordering remains unchanged.
tenant-a-fast-release-event-ticketing.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence shows the policy in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory and fork CI still passes.

tenant-a-processor-fee-alignment.description: Tenant A aligns checkout totals to its processor contract.
tenant-a-processor-fee-alignment.impact: Fee display and totals change while payment, ticket issuance, refund, and check-in behavior remain fixed.
tenant-a-processor-fee-alignment.testable: T112 fee tests still prove inline-pure calculation, and tenant visual evidence shows the adapted checkout total.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T99 through T118
Payment and refund record persistence before emitted events
Ticket issuance and QR validation persistence
PENDING as a valid private booking state
CloudEvents envelope usage where events cross service boundaries

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a TYPE-A ticketing policy or presentation change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted policy is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
Tenant A visual evidence shows the adapted checkout or ticketing policy.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
